channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: UCD Ad Astra Doctoral Scholarships 2026 fully funded PhD in digital education and wellbeing
item.dc:creator: Michelle Zyongwe
item.description:
The UCD Ad Astra Doctoral Scholarships 2026 are now open at the University College Dublin (UCD) School of Education. Two fully funded four-year PhD scholarships are available for outstanding candidates interested in researching the impact of digital technologies on education and children’s lives. With a generous stipend, full fee waiver, and research funding, the UCD […]

The post UCD Ad Astra Doctoral Scholarships 2026 fully funded PhD in digital education and wellbeing appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The UCD Ad Astra Doctoral Scholarships 2026 are now open at the University College Dublin (UCD) School of Education. Two fully funded four-year PhD scholarships are available for outstanding candidates interested in researching the impact of digital technologies on education and children’s lives.

With a generous stipend, full fee waiver, and research funding, the UCD Ad Astra Doctoral Scholarships offer an exceptional opportunity for doctoral researchers to develop innovative projects in critical edtech studies or digital wellbeing in childhood and adolescence.

📅 Application Deadline: 31 March 2026

📍 Start Date: September 2026

🎤 Interviews: Second half of April 2026

Scholarship Overview

The two scholarships are hosted within the UCD School of Education and linked to leading research centres:

  • UCD Centre for Cyber Resilience Education
  • UCD Centre for the Advancement of Wellbeing in Education

Each scholarship provides:

  • €25,000 annual tax-free stipend
  • Full PhD tuition fee waiver (EU and non-EU students eligible)
  • €4,000 annual research budget (conference travel, training, data collection, equipment, etc.)
  • Four years of funding (subject to satisfactory progression)

Successful candidates must enrol full-time in either:

  • Education and Lifelong Learning PhD
  • Children and Youth Studies PhD

1️⃣ Ad Astra PhD: Critical Edtech Studies

This scholarship focuses on the pedagogical, ethical, and experiential dimensions of digital technologies in education.

Supervision: Dr Michał Wieczorek

Centre: UCD Centre for Cyber Resilience Education

Applicants are encouraged to design their own research projects aligned with critical educational technology studies. Both philosophical and qualitative approaches are welcome, especially projects combining theoretical and empirical work.

Possible Research Themes

  • Ethical issues in edtech (ethics of care, feminist, non-Western frameworks)
  • Pedagogical theories embedded in educational platforms
  • Edtech’s impact on teachers’ labour and classroom practice
  • Political economy of big tech in education
  • Commercial vs. public actors in educational technology
  • Historical analysis of technology adoption in schools
  • Children’s experiences of platforms such as Minecraft, Roblox, or Lego
  • Affective dimensions of generative AI use in education

Candidates with backgrounds in philosophy, sociology, education studies, science and technology studies, or communication studies are especially encouraged to apply.

2️⃣ Ad Astra PhD: Digital Wellbeing in Childhood & Adolescence

This scholarship supports original quantitative or mixed-methods research examining the relationship between digital technology use and child and adolescent wellbeing.

Supervision: Dr Melissa Bohnert

Centre: UCD Centre for the Advancement of Wellbeing in Education

Projects should explore associations between digital engagement and wellbeing outcomes, with attention to social relationships and structural inequalities.

Preferred Approaches

  • Longitudinal data analysis
  • Advanced statistical modelling
  • Mixed-methods integration
  • Causal inference
  • Mediation and moderation models
  • Social network analysis

Example Research Topics

  • Digital media use and youth mental health outcomes
  • Developmental trajectories of digital engagement
  • Socioeconomic inequalities and digital wellbeing
  • Peer, family, and school support systems
  • Digital parenting styles
  • Comparative and cross-cohort analyses
  • Measuring digital behaviour and wellbeing innovation

Applicants with backgrounds in sociology, psychology, demography, education studies, or related disciplines — and strong quantitative skills — are strongly encouraged to apply.

Eligibility & Expectations

Successful candidates must:

  • Enrol full-time in September 2026
  • Apply to only one of the two scholarships
  • Demonstrate methodological alignment with the selected track
  • Meet English language requirements (unless exempt)

Experience working with children or teaching is considered an advantage for both tracks.

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit:

  • One-page cover letter
  • Two-page research proposal pitch (excluding bibliography)
  • CV (including contact details for two referees)
  • Writing sample
  • Academic transcripts
  • Evidence of English proficiency (if required)

📅 Deadline: 31 March 2026

APPLY HERE

Why Apply for the UCD Ad Astra Doctoral Scholarships?

These prestigious scholarships offer:

✔ Generous tax-free stipend

✔ Full tuition coverage

✔ Annual research funding

✔ Strong interdisciplinary supervision

✔ Access to international research networks

✔ Opportunity to shape debates on digital education and youth wellbeing

The UCD Ad Astra Doctoral Scholarships 2026 are ideal for ambitious researchers seeking to contribute meaningfully to global conversations on digital transformation, education policy, and child wellbeing.

For more fully funded PhD opportunities and scholarships worldwide, visit Here

The post UCD Ad Astra Doctoral Scholarships 2026 fully funded PhD in digital education and wellbeing appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: EUI Visit 2026: Funded PhD Research at European University Institute
item.dc:creator: Michelle Zyongwe
item.description:
The EUI Visit 2026 under the European University Institute Widening Europe Programme is now open for PhD researchers from eligible widening countries. This EUI Visit provides a funded opportunity to spend the Autumn term of the 2026–2027 academic year at one of Europe’s leading postgraduate institutions. Through the EUI Visit 2026, selected doctoral researchers will […]

The post EUI Visit 2026: Funded PhD Research at European University Institute appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The EUI Visit 2026 under the European University Institute Widening Europe Programme is now open for PhD researchers from eligible widening countries. This EUI Visit provides a funded opportunity to spend the Autumn term of the 2026–2027 academic year at one of Europe’s leading postgraduate institutions. Through the EUI Visit 2026, selected doctoral researchers will join a dynamic academic environment, collaborate with distinguished faculty, and advance their PhD research in Florence, Italy.

The call was announced on 17 February 2026 as part of the Widening Programme’s continued efforts to strengthen research excellence and internationalisation across Europe.

 

About the EUI Visit Programme

The EUI Visit is part of the EUI Widening Europe Programme, an initiative designed to support doctoral researchers from targeted Widening countries by providing access to high-level academic collaboration and research infrastructure.

The programme aims to:

•Strengthen research capacity in widening countries

•Promote academic collaboration across Europe

•Improve competitiveness and research quality

•Foster a more cohesive European Higher Education and Research Area

Selected participants will conduct research at the European University Institute (EUI), located in Florence, Italy — a globally recognized hub for advanced research in the social sciences and humanities.

 

Departments Available Under the EUI Visit 2026

PhD researchers selected for the EUI Visit 2026 will join one of the following departments:

•Department of Political and Social Sciences

•Department of History

•Department of Law

During their stay, visiting researchers will:

•Work closely with EUI faculty members

•Advance their doctoral dissertation research

•Participate in seminars, workshops, and academic discussions

•Engage with an international community of scholars

The EUI Visit provides a unique opportunity to deepen research work within a stimulating interdisciplinary academic environment.

 

Benefits of the EUI Visit 2026

The EUI Visit 2026 offers several important academic and professional benefits:

1. Funded Research Stay

The programme provides funding support for selected researchers during their stay at EUI.

2. Academic Mentorship

Participants collaborate directly with experienced faculty members who provide academic guidance and feedback.

3. International Research Network

The EUI hosts scholars from around the world, allowing visiting PhD students to expand their academic networks.

4. Access to Research Infrastructure

Researchers gain access to EUI’s extensive library resources, research facilities, and academic events.

5. Enhanced Academic Profile

Spending a term at a prestigious European institution strengthens a PhD candidate’s academic credentials.

 

Eligibility Criteria for the EUI Visit

To apply for the EUI Visit 2026, candidates must meet the following requirements:

•Be enrolled in a PhD programme at a higher education institution or public research centre in a targeted Widening country.

•Hold nationality from one of the eligible Widening countries listed below.

Eligible EU Countries:

Bulgaria, Croatia, Cyprus, Czechia, Estonia, Greece, Hungary, Latvia, Lithuania, Malta, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Slovakia, Slovenia.

Eligible Non-EU Countries:

Albania, Bosnia & Herzegovina, Georgia, Kosovo*, Moldova, Montenegro, North Macedonia, Serbia, Ukraine.

Applicants must ensure their doctoral research aligns with the academic expertise of the department they are applying to.

 

Application Deadlines

Deadlines vary depending on the department:

•Department of Political and Social Sciences: 31 March 2026

•Department of History: 30 April 2026

•Department of Law: 1 May 2026

Applicants are strongly encouraged to review the relevant departmental webpages for full eligibility details and application instructions.

 

Why Apply for the EUI Visit?

The EUI Visit 2026 is more than a short-term research stay. It is an opportunity to:

•Refine your doctoral research with expert mentorship

•Gain international academic exposure

•Engage in interdisciplinary scholarly exchange

•Strengthen your future academic and professional prospects

For PhD students from widening countries, this programme represents a strategic opportunity to expand research impact and collaborate within a top-tier European academic institution.

APPLY HERE

 

Final Note

The EUI Visit 2026 is a highly competitive and prestigious opportunity for doctoral researchers seeking international academic collaboration. If you are a PhD candidate from an eligible Widening country, this funded research visit can significantly enhance your doctoral journey.

For more fully funded research programmes, scholarships, and fellowships worldwide, click here

 

The post EUI Visit 2026: Funded PhD Research at European University Institute appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Living Lands Charter Internships 2026 (x2) at the Commonwealth Secretariat – Paid Climate Change Internship in London
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Looking for a paid climate change internship in the UK? Applications are now open for the Living Lands Charter Internships (x2) at the Commonwealth Secretariat, offering young professionals the opportunity to work on global climate, biodiversity, and sustainable land management initiatives. This six-month internship provides hands-on experience within an international policy environment while supporting implementation […]

The post Living Lands Charter Internships 2026 (x2) at the Commonwealth Secretariat – Paid Climate Change Internship in London appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Looking for a paid climate change internship in the UK? Applications are now open for the Living Lands Charter Internships (x2) at the Commonwealth Secretariat, offering young professionals the opportunity to work on global climate, biodiversity, and sustainable land management initiatives.

This six-month internship provides hands-on experience within an international policy environment while supporting implementation of the Commonwealth Living Lands Charter, a major global commitment adopted at the Commonwealth Heads of Government Meeting (CHOGM) in Kigali, Rwanda.

About the Living Lands Charter Internship

The Living Lands Charter Internship sits within the Climate Change Section of the Climate Change and Oceans Division at the Commonwealth Secretariat in London.

The Commonwealth Living Lands Charter was adopted in June 2022 and represents a shared commitment by all 56 Commonwealth member countries to:

  • Address climate change, biodiversity loss, and land degradation

  • Promote climate-resilient and sustainable land management

  • Strengthen global collaboration on environmental protection

  • Support inclusive and evidence-based policy solutions

Interns will gain exposure to international development, environmental governance, and climate policy while contributing to real-world initiatives impacting countries across the Commonwealth.

Internship Details

Position: Living Lands Charter Intern (2 positions)
Duration: 6 months
Location: London, United Kingdom
Salary: £498.60 per week
Organisation: Commonwealth Secretariat

This internship offers a professional international environment where selected candidates will build networks, develop technical skills, and gain valuable experience in climate and environmental policy.

Key Responsibilities

Under the supervision of the Adviser – Climate Change, interns will:

  • Support research across key Living Lands Charter themes including:

    • Agriculture

    • Water and soil conservation

    • Climate and health

    • Biodiversity and green development

    • Indigenous peoples and sustainable land use

  • Monitor media and academic developments related to climate change and land management

  • Help promote awareness and visibility of the Living Lands Charter

  • Support updates to databases and the Living Lands micro-website

  • Contribute to policy and governance research related to climate and biodiversity financing and capacity building

Eligibility Criteria

To apply, candidates must:

  • Be 20–30 years old

  • Be currently enrolled in or undertaking a postgraduate degree in:

    • Agriculture

    • Biodiversity

    • Ecology

    • Animal Science

    • Soil Science

    • Or a related field

  • Be a citizen of a Commonwealth country

  • Have the right to live and work in the UK for the full internship duration

Why Apply?

This internship is ideal for young professionals seeking experience in:

  • Climate change policy

  • Sustainable land management

  • Environmental research and governance

  • International development and diplomacy

Benefits include:

  • Weekly stipend (£498.60)

  • Exposure to global climate and biodiversity initiatives

  • Professional networking opportunities

  • Experience within an international intergovernmental organisation

About the Commonwealth Secretariat

The Commonwealth Secretariat is the intergovernmental organisation that supports member countries to achieve the Commonwealth’s aims of development, democracy and peace.

Members:

  • are a voice for small and vulnerable states
  • champion young people
  • promote justice and human rights
  • address threats like climate change, debt and inequality
  • help grow economies and boost trade
  • support decision-makers to make good laws and deliver policies
  • help strengthen governance and build inclusive public institutions
  • provide technical help and training
  • send experts and observers to countries to give impartial advice and help solve national problems
  • provide systems, software and research for managing resources.

Commonwealth Member Countries (56):
Antigua and Barbuda, Australia, Bahamas, Bangladesh, Barbados, Belize, Botswana, Brunei Darussalam, Cameroon, Canada, Cyprus, Dominica, Eswatini, Fiji, Gabon, Gambia, Ghana, Grenada, Guyana, India, Jamaica, Kenya, Kiribati, Lesotho, Malawi, Malaysia, Maldives, Malta, Mauritius, Mozambique, Namibia, Nauru, New Zealand, Nigeria, Pakistan, Papua New Guinea, Rwanda, Saint Kitts and Nevis, Saint Lucia, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines, Samoa, Seychelles, Sierra Leone, Singapore, Solomon Islands, South Africa, Sri Lanka, Tanzania, Togo, Tonga, Trinidad and Tobago, Tuvalu, Uganda, United Kingdom, Vanuatu, Zambia.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should submit:

  • A CV

  • A cover letter addressing key criteria in the person specification with clear examples

Supporting documents include:

  • Living Lands Charter Internship Job Description

  • Application Guidelines

  • Internship Policy

 

APPLY NOW

If you are passionate about climate action, biodiversity, and sustainable development, this is an excellent opportunity to gain real-world international experience while contributing to global environmental solutions.

Early applications are strongly encouraged.

The post Living Lands Charter Internships 2026 (x2) at the Commonwealth Secretariat – Paid Climate Change Internship in London appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Vanier Institute Conference Funding 2026 – Financial Support for Graduate Students and Postdoctoral Fellows in Family Science
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Are you a master’s student, doctoral candidate, or postdoctoral fellow working in family science? The Vanier Institute of the Family Conference Funding 2026 program is now open for applications. This funding opportunity is designed to support graduate students and postdoctoral researchers who are actively engaged in family science knowledge exchange and wish to participate in […]

The post Vanier Institute Conference Funding 2026 – Financial Support for Graduate Students and Postdoctoral Fellows in Family Science appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you a master’s student, doctoral candidate, or postdoctoral fellow working in family science? The Vanier Institute of the Family Conference Funding 2026 program is now open for applications.

This funding opportunity is designed to support graduate students and postdoctoral researchers who are actively engaged in family science knowledge exchange and wish to participate in academic conferences. If you are presenting research, sharing findings, or contributing to discussions that advance the understanding of families and social wellbeing, this program can help offset your participation costs.

About the Vanier Institute of the Family

The Vanier Institute of the Family is a Canadian organization dedicated to advancing knowledge and research about families. Through research dissemination and knowledge exchange initiatives, the Institute supports evidence-based dialogue that strengthens family wellbeing and policy development.

This conference funding initiative reflects the Institute’s commitment to supporting emerging scholars and researchers in the field of family science.

What the Conference Funding Covers

The funding supports participation in conferences related to family science knowledge exchange. While specific amounts and allowable expenses should be confirmed on the official page, support typically contributes toward:

  • Conference registration fees

  • Travel expenses

  • Accommodation costs

  • Knowledge dissemination activities

Applicants should carefully review eligible expenses and funding limits before applying.

Why You Should Apply

Participating in academic conferences provides valuable opportunities to:

  • Present your research to an international audience

  • Expand your academic and professional network

  • Receive feedback from experts in the field

  • Strengthen your CV and academic profile

  • Increase the visibility and impact of your research

This funding can reduce financial barriers and help you maximize these professional development opportunities.

Who Is Eligible?

The funding is available to:

  • Master’s students

  • Doctoral students

  • Postdoctoral fellows

Applicants must be engaged in family science research and knowledge exchange activities.

Application Timeline

Applications for the 2026 cycle are now open. Applicants are strongly encouraged to review the official page for specific submission deadlines and funding timelines, as late applications may not be considered.

Final Thoughts

Conference participation plays a critical role in shaping academic careers, particularly for early-career researchers. If your work contributes to advancing family science, this funding opportunity can help you share your insights, strengthen collaborations, and contribute meaningfully to research-informed dialogue.

                                      How to Apply

Interested applicants should review the detailed eligibility requirements and submit their application via the official link.

For more OFY conferences opportunities, Click Here.

The post Vanier Institute Conference Funding 2026 – Financial Support for Graduate Students and Postdoctoral Fellows in Family Science appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Visiting PhD Fellowship 2026 at United Nations University-WIDER — Fully Funded Research Opportunity for PhD Students
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The UNU-WIDER Visiting PhD Fellowship Programme 2026 offers doctoral students an opportunity to strengthen their PhD research while working with one of the world’s leading development economics institutes. This fully funded fellowship allows researchers to access UNU-WIDER’s resources, collaborate with experts, and advance research focused on developing economies. For PhD candidates seeking international research exposure, […]

The post Visiting PhD Fellowship 2026 at United Nations University-WIDER — Fully Funded Research Opportunity for PhD Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The UNU-WIDER Visiting PhD Fellowship Programme 2026 offers doctoral students an opportunity to strengthen their PhD research while working with one of the world’s leading development economics institutes. This fully funded fellowship allows researchers to access UNU-WIDER’s resources, collaborate with experts, and advance research focused on developing economies.

For PhD candidates seeking international research exposure, publication opportunities, and academic networking, this programme provides a strong platform to enhance both dissertation quality and career development.

Visiting PhD fellows typically spend three consecutive months at UNU-WIDER before returning to their home institution. During the programme, fellows prepare one or more research papers and present a seminar on their research findings. They may also have the opportunity to publish their research in the WIDER Working Paper Series.

You can read about previous experiences from the programme, here.

About the UNU-WIDER Visiting PhD Fellowship Programme

The Visiting PhD Fellowship Programme enables registered doctoral students to:

  • Use UNU-WIDER’s research resources and facilities

  • Collaborate with researchers on shared research interests

  • Develop research papers related to their dissertation or thesis

  • Present a seminar based on their research findings

  • Potentially publish in the WIDER Working Paper Series

Fellows usually spend three consecutive months at UNU-WIDER before returning to their home institution.

Fellowship Duration and Research Activities

During the three-month fellowship period, participants are expected to:

  • Advance their doctoral research

  • Engage with UNU-WIDER researchers and academic discussions

  • Prepare research outputs suitable for publication

  • Present their research in a seminar setting

The programme is designed to strengthen research quality and support policy-relevant academic work.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must:

  • Be currently enrolled in a PhD programme

  • Demonstrate ability to conduct research on developing economies

  • Possess strong quantitative and/or qualitative analytical skills

  • Be fluent in written and spoken English

Although UNU-WIDER is an economics-focused institute, researchers from other social science disciplines may apply if their work aligns with development-related themes.

Submissions on topics related to the UNU-WIDER work programme are particularly welcome.

Priority Applicants

Applications are especially encouraged from:

  • Early-career researchers

  • Female researchers

  • Researchers from developing countries

  • PhD candidates at later stages of their studies

The programme is highly competitive. In recent years, approximately one percent of applications were successful.

Financial Support

When the fellowship is undertaken in Helsinki, UNU-WIDER provides:

  • A travel grant covering travel to and from the applicant’s institution or country of residence

  • Medical insurance during the fellowship period

  • A monthly stipend of EUR 2,070 to support living expenses in Helsinki

The programme does not cover expenses related to dependents.

Application Timeline and Deadlines

UNU-WIDER accepts applications twice each year.

March Application Cycle

  • Application form opens: 1 March (00:00 UTC+3)

  • Deadline: 31 March (23:59 UTC+3)

  • Fellowship period: September to November (same year)

September Application Cycle

  • Application form opens: 1 September (00:00 UTC+3)

  • Deadline: 30 September (23:59 UTC+3)

  • Fellowship period: March to June (following year)

The online application link becomes active one month before each deadline.

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit:

  • Completed online application form

  • Updated curriculum vitae (CV)

  • Reference letter from PhD supervisor

The reference letter must be sent directly by the supervisor to:

phdreference@wider.unu.edu

This letter also confirms the applicant’s enrolment in a PhD programme.

Applications submitted by email or post are not accepted.

Selection Process

  • Applications are assessed competitively based on eligibility and research alignment

  • Final decisions are communicated by email within four weeks after the deadline

  • Selected applicants must confirm acceptance within two weeks of notification

Why Apply for the UNU-WIDER Visiting PhD Fellowship?

This fellowship is ideal for doctoral researchers who want to:

  • Strengthen their dissertation with international research support

  • Work closely with leading development economists

  • Build academic and policy research networks

  • Increase opportunities for publication

  • Enhance future academic and professional prospects

How to Apply

Interested candidates should complete the online application form once it becomes available during the application window.

If you are interested in participating in this programme you should complete and submit the application form. For more details on the submission and for practical information on the programme, please see our frequently asked questions page.

Contact Information

If you have questions about the programme or you experience any problems with your online application, please first check our questions and answers page. If you are still experiencing problems please contact us at the following email address: phdfellow@wider.unu.edu

Please note that replies may take up to five working days.

The UNU-WIDER Visiting PhD Fellowship 2026 is a highly competitive and valuable opportunity for doctoral students conducting research on developing economies. With financial support, access to expert researchers, and opportunities to publish, the programme provides a strong environment for advancing high-quality, policy-relevant research.

FIND OUT MORE AND APPLY HERE

The Visiting PhD Fellowship Programme was previously known as the PhD Internship Programme.

QUICK LINKS

Learning

Previous experiences

Frequently asked questions – Visiting PhD Fellowship

The post Visiting PhD Fellowship 2026 at United Nations University-WIDER — Fully Funded Research Opportunity for PhD Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: 3rd Global MAB Youth Forum 2026 — Fully Funded UNESCO Youth Forum in Italy (Apply Now)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Key Details Organizer: UNESCO Man and the Biosphere (MAB) Programme Location: Rosolina Mare, Italy Dates: 18–22 May 2026 Format: In-person Age range: 18–35 years Maximum participants: 200 Theme: Committed to the Future We Want Funding: Accommodation and meals covered; limited travel support available Application deadline: 23 March 2026 (23:59 CET) Contact: mab@unesco.org The third Global […]

The post 3rd Global MAB Youth Forum 2026 — Fully Funded UNESCO Youth Forum in Italy (Apply Now) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Key Details

  • Organizer: UNESCO Man and the Biosphere (MAB) Programme

  • Location: Rosolina Mare, Italy

  • Dates: 18–22 May 2026

  • Format: In-person

  • Age range: 18–35 years

  • Maximum participants: 200

  • Theme: Committed to the Future We Want

  • Funding: Accommodation and meals covered; limited travel support available

  • Application deadline: 23 March 2026 (23:59 CET)

  • Contact: mab@unesco.org

The third Global MAB Youth Forum marks a key milestone for strengthening youth participation in UNESCO’s Man and the Biosphere (MAB) Programme.
UNESCO 18 May 2026 – 22 May 2026

Theme

Building on the 2017 and 2019 Global MAB Youth Forums, the third forum adopts the theme “Committed to the Future We Want.” Aligned with the Hangzhou Strategic Action Plan (2026–2035), it reflects MAB Youth’s strengthened commitment to addressing biodiversity loss, climate change, and sustainable development. 

Objectives

The forum aims to: 

  • Enable MAB Youth to engage with and contribute to the implementation of the Hangzhou Strategic Action Plan (2026–2035), with particular attention to Action Target 20 on developing the MAB Youth Network. 
  • Translate findings from the World MAB Youth Mapping Survey into shared priorities, innovative approaches, and concrete pathways for action. 
  • Facilitate the exchange of youth-led practices and initiatives in Biosphere Reserves related to biodiversity conservation, sustainable development, and community well-being. 
  • Foster coordination, leadership, and capacity-building among youth actors across the World Network of Biosphere Reserves. 
  • Produce concrete youth-led outputs and recommendations that inform MAB governance and future action at national, regional, and global levels. 
  • Identify strategies to strengthen the integration of youth within MAB governance structures and decision-making.  

Who can apply

The Forum is intended for young people aged 18–35 who are actively engaged in or connected to UNESCO Biosphere Reserves (e.g., living, working, studying, volunteering, conducting research, or supporting BR-related activities). The maximum number of participants is 200. The Call for Expression of Interest will close on 23 March 2026 at 23:59 CET. 

Two distinct application pathways are available: 

  1. National MAB Youth Focal Points: Designated National MAB Youth Focal Points are invited to apply as official representatives within the framework of the MAB Programme. In view of their official national designation, National MAB Youth Focal Points who submit the required documentation shall be eligible to participate in the Forum without being subject to a competitive selection process.
  2. MAB Youth Participants (Open Call – Other than National MAB Youth Focal Points): Eligible young people aged 18–35 who are actively engaged in or connected to UNESCO Biosphere Reserves (e.g., living, working, studying, volunteering, conducting research, or supporting BR-related activities) may apply through an open Expression of Interest process. Selection will be conducted competitively based on merit, geographical balance, gender parity, and relevance to the Forum’s objectives. 

How to apply

Applicants should submit an online expression of interest through the application form(s). The application should include basic information and, depending on the category, supporting documents.  

National MAB Youth Focal Points

National MAB Youth Focal Points are requested to submit the following documents:  

  1. A confirmation letter of designation issued by the MAB Secretariat, confirming their official appointment as National MAB Youth Focal Point. 
  2. A short description of their experience, initiatives, or engagement related to UNESCO Biosphere Reserves, including their role and key outcomes.  
    (Max. 250 words) 
  3. A brief statement of contribution (max. 250 words) outlining what they can bring to the 3rd MAB Youth Forum in their role as National MAB Youth Focal Point. 
  4. A brief statement (max. 250 words) explaining how they plan to follow up at national or regional levels after the Forum.
  5. An indication of whether they have confirmed financial support to cover their travel costs. If yes, supporting documentation may be uploaded.  

APPLY 

MAB Youth Participants (open call)

Applicants are requested to submit the following: 

  1. A recent Curriculum Vitae (CV). 
  2. A short description of their experience, initiatives, or engagement related to UNESCO Biosphere Reserves, including their role and key outcomes. (Max. 250 words) 
  3. A statement outlining the contributions they intend to bring to the Forum and the outcomes they hope to achieve. (Max. 250 words) 
  4. A brief explanation of how they plan to apply the outcomes of the Forum in their Biosphere Reserve, community, or country after the event. 
    (Max. 250 words) 
  5. A letter of endorsement or support from a Biosphere Reserve or respective MAB National Committee or National Commission for UNESCO.
  6. An indication of whether they have confirmed financial support to cover their travel costs. If yes, supporting documentation may be uploaded. 

APPLY 

Travel and financial arrangements

Food and accommodation costs in Italy during the Forum will generously be covered by the organizers. Limited financial support may be provided to assist participants with travel expenses, depending on funding availability and eligibility criteria. 

Organisers

  • Po Delta Biosphere Reserve
  • Colli Euganei Biosphere Reserve
  • Ministry of the Environment and Energy Security
  • Ministry of Foreign Affairs and International Cooperation
  • Fondazione Cassa di Risparmio di Padova e Rovigo
  • Italian National Commission for UNESCO
  • UNESCO MAB Secretariat
  • UNESCO Regional Bureau for Science and Culture in Europe
  • Veneto Region

Contact

UNESCO MAB Secretariat: mab@unesco.org 

Useful links

Man and the Biosphere Programme
UNESCO Venice
Po Delta Biosphere Reserve
Colli Euganei Biosphere Reserve

Source for more info: https://www.unesco.org/en/articles/3rd-global-mab-youth-forum-2026-committed-future-we-want

The post 3rd Global MAB Youth Forum 2026 — Fully Funded UNESCO Youth Forum in Italy (Apply Now) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply Now: Junior Collaborator – Institutional Engagement and Development Actors (Remote, IIHL)
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
Are you passionate about international law, humanitarian protection and advancing sustainable development? The Apply for Junior Collaborator – Institutional Engagement & Development Actors (IIHL) represents a unique opportunity to join a world-renowned organisation shaping global policy and partnerships in refugee law and migration law. Position Title – Junior Collaborator – Institutional Engagement & Development Actors– […]

The post Apply Now: Junior Collaborator – Institutional Engagement and Development Actors (Remote, IIHL) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you passionate about international law, humanitarian protection and advancing sustainable development? The Apply for Junior Collaborator – Institutional Engagement & Development Actors (IIHL) represents a unique opportunity to join a world-renowned organisation shaping global policy and partnerships in refugee law and migration law.

Position Title

– Junior Collaborator – Institutional Engagement & Development Actors
– Department of International Refugee Law and Migration Law
– International Institute of Humanitarian Law (IIHL), Sanremo, Italy (Remote)

Application Deadline

Applications Close on 5th March 2026 (Rome CET time)

Work Location

Remote / Home-based (with reliable internet)
Travel to Sanremo (Italy), Geneva (Switzerland) or other duty stations may be required and fully covered by IIHL.

Contract Details

  • Contract Type: 6-month collaboration contract

  • Remuneration: ~€500 per month

  • Scholarships: Complimentary access to Department’s online learning courses

  • Hours: Full-time remote role with possible attendance at meetings in Rome CET time zone

Role Overview

Under guidance from the Head of the Institutional Engagement Unit, the Junior Collaborator will:

• Conduct research on development actors and humanitarian–development–peace nexus issues
• Map partnership and funding opportunities with global development institutions
• Support online and in-person capacity-sharing and learning initiatives
• Track partnerships and contribute to key strategic work of the Department

This role is ideal for early-career professionals seeking first-hand experience with international organisations and a gateway to future roles in humanitarian law and development sectors.

Key Responsibilities

1. Mapping & Research

  • Research development actors including governmental, UN, regional, and financial institutions

  • Identify partnership and funding possibilities for capacity building and strategic projects.

  • Analyze trends in protection, displacement, and sustainable responses.

2. Support & Outreach

  • Support capacity-sharing initiatives like online courses.

  • Maintain and update the Department’s internal partnerships tracker.

  • Assist with documentation and reporting on development engagement activities.

Qualifications & Skills Required

Mandatory
1. University degree in Social Sciences, International Relations, Political Science, Economics, Development Studies, or related field.
2. Strong organizational, written, and oral communication skills.
3. Fluency in English (other UN languages are a plus).
4. Commitment to humanitarian and protection work in displacement contexts.

Desirable
1. Experience with international development projects or institutions.
2. Familiarity with refugee, migration, and statelessness law frameworks.
3. Knowledge of instructional design or online capacity-building platforms.

Why This Opportunity Matters

This role is strategically positioned at the intersection of humanitarian action, development, and global partnerships. By focusing on development actors, you’ll engage with institutions shaping responses to displacement, resilience, and global cooperation — all while enhancing your professional credentials in a globally respected institution.

Benefits of the Position

• Professional experience with an international legal & humanitarian organisation
• Exposure to global development and refugee protection coordination
• Career development through learning scholarships
• Remote flexibility with optional travel fully funded by IIHL
• Priority consideration for future roles within the Department

How to Apply

Submit the following through the official IIHL Careers Portal:

 Required Documents:
1. Updated CV
2. Motivation Letter

Apply here

Tip: The Institute may start interviews before the deadline if strong candidates are identified early.

The International Institute of Humanitarian Law embraces diversity and inclusion — all qualified applicants are encouraged to apply regardless of gender, nationality, ethnicity, religion, disability, age, sexual orientation, or background. Applications from individuals with forced displacement and statelessness experiences are especially welcome.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Apply Now: Junior Collaborator – Institutional Engagement and Development Actors (Remote, IIHL) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply now for the PhD Programme in Economics at the Cologne Graduate School (CGS), University of Cologne
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
If you’re an ambitious researcher looking to pursue a world-class PhD in Economics, the Cologne Graduate School in Management, Economics and Social Sciences (CGS) at the University of Cologne (UoC) is one of the most competitive and rewarding graduate destinations in Europe. Why This Programme is a Top Global Choice The PhD Programme in Economics […]

The post Apply now for the PhD Programme in Economics at the Cologne Graduate School (CGS), University of Cologne appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you’re an ambitious researcher looking to pursue a world-class PhD in Economics, the Cologne Graduate School in Management, Economics and Social Sciences (CGS) at the University of Cologne (UoC) is one of the most competitive and rewarding graduate destinations in Europe.

Why This Programme is a Top Global Choice

The PhD Programme in Economics at CGS/UoC stands out because it offers:

  • A fully English-taught, research-driven curriculum with international faculty.

  • Rigorous training that prepares you for academic careers and top research jobs worldwide.

  • Structured supervision and research opportunities across economics, econometrics, political economy, behavioral economics, and more.

  • A vibrant research ecosystem with connections to major research centers like the Cluster of Excellence “ECONtribute: Markets & Public Policy” and external partners like the Max Planck Institute.

  • No tuition and multiple funding avenues including fully funded PhD positions, scholarships, and research assistant roles.

Funding, Scholarships & Benefits

1. Full PhD Funding

  • Fully funded doctoral positions through CGS or partners.

  • Funding typically covers living expenses during the PhD stage.

2. Stipends for M.Sc. Stage

  • A limited number of scholarships available for exceptional M.Sc. students.

Who Can Apply: Eligibility & Qualifications

 1. Applicants with a Bachelor’s Degree

You can enter the programme via the M.Sc. Economic Research (First Stage), designed to bridge undergraduate preparation to doctoral research.
Ideal if:

  • Your Bachelor’s is in Economics, Econometrics, Finance, or related fields.

  • You rank among the top graduates in your cohort.

Outcome:Completing the M.Sc. successfully gives you direct access to the doctoral (PhD) stage.

 2. Applicants with a Master’s Degree

Students holding a master’s degree in Economics or a closely related discipline can apply directly to the PhD Stage.

Strong qualification signals include:

  • Proven research interest or thesis evidence.

  • Academic excellence (top GPA/grades).

  • Clear match between your research interests and faculty expertise.

Additional Training or Course Work

  • If your Master’s does not include sufficient Economics coursework, you may need up to one year of additional courses drawn from the M.Sc. curriculum.

Application Checklist & Submission Requirements

To build a strong application, prepare the following:

Standard Application Materials

  •  Certified degree transcripts and diplomas (Bachelor’s/Master’s)

  •  Detailed CV with academic highlights.

  •  Research proposal (ideally aligned with faculty research)

  •  Recommendation letters (academic)

  •  Motivation letter explaining your fit for research.

  •  Proof of English proficiency (if not from an English-medium degree). (site may specify requirements when applications open)

Apply here

Application Deadlines & Intake Periods

 PhD Programme (with Integrated M.Sc. Option)

  • 15th March 2026 for PhD

  • 31st March 2026 for Msc

  •  Programme starts every October

Tip: Mark mid-January to early February in your calendar to check for open application calls.

Strategic Tips to Win Admission

• Start Preparing Early – applications open January; deadlines are strict.
• Match faculty research – show clear alignment between your research vision and CGS strengths.
• Solid research proposal boosts competitiveness in this highly selective programme.
• Strong academic record is essential, especially at the master’s level.

Other Funding Opportunities

  • External fellowships such as EWI Fellowship for Energy Economics (requiring separate application).

  • Some doctoral positions are offered jointly with partner research institutions (e.g Max Planck Institute).

Additional Perks

  • Extensive conference funding and research stay support.

  • Opportunities for joint publications and collaborative research.

  • No tuition fees — only a nominal semester fee

The PhD Programme in Economics at the Cologne Graduate School is a globally respected research destination — and for good reason. With no tuition, funded PhD positions, a strong international research network, and excellent post-graduate prospects, it’s a transformative step for future economists and academic leaders.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Apply now for the PhD Programme in Economics at the Cologne Graduate School (CGS), University of Cologne appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Carter Center Educational Programs 2026: Paid Internships and Graduate Assistantships (Complete Guide)
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Key Details Organization: The Carter CenterLocation: Atlanta, Georgia, USA (in-person)Programs: Paid Internships and Graduate AssistantshipsFocus Areas: Peace, Health, Democracy, Human Rights, International Development, OperationsInternship Stipend: Spring (15 weeks): $5,100 Summer (10 weeks): $3,400 Fall (15 weeks): $5,100 Graduate Assistantship Compensation: $18.50 per hour 9–12 months, 20 hours per week Internship Deadlines: Summer 2026: March 2, […]

The post Carter Center Educational Programs 2026: Paid Internships and Graduate Assistantships (Complete Guide) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Key Details

Organization: The Carter Center
Location: Atlanta, Georgia, USA (in-person)
Programs: Paid Internships and Graduate Assistantships
Focus Areas: Peace, Health, Democracy, Human Rights, International Development, Operations
Internship Stipend:

  • Spring (15 weeks): $5,100

  • Summer (10 weeks): $3,400

  • Fall (15 weeks): $5,100

Graduate Assistantship Compensation:

  • $18.50 per hour

  • 9–12 months, 20 hours per week

Internship Deadlines:

  • Summer 2026: March 2, 2026 (11:59 p.m. ET)

  • Fall 2026: June 15, 2026 (11:59 p.m. ET)

Graduate Assistantship Deadlines:

  • Application Opens: February 13, 2026

  • Application Deadline: April 23, 2026 (11:59 p.m. ET)

  • Recommendation Letters Deadline: April 30, 2026

Who Can Apply:

  • Undergraduate juniors or seniors

  • Graduate students

  • Recent graduates (within 24 months)

  • International students authorized to work in the U.S.

Introduction

The Carter Center Educational Programs provide structured professional opportunities for students and early-career professionals interested in advancing global peace, public health, democracy, and operational excellence. With more than four decades of experience and nearly 4,000 participants worldwide, the program has become a recognized pathway for individuals pursuing careers in international development, policy, and nonprofit leadership.

Through paid internships and graduate assistantships, participants gain hands-on experience supporting real projects while building practical skills, professional networks, and career direction.

About the Carter Center Educational Programs

The Educational Programs were created to cultivate informed and committed leaders who can contribute meaningfully to global peace and health initiatives. Participants work alongside experts and program teams, applying academic knowledge to practical challenges while gaining exposure to the realities of international development work.

The programs emphasize professional growth, mentorship, and experiential learning, helping participants explore career pathways and strengthen technical and leadership competencies.

Carter Center Internship Program

Program Overview

The Carter Center Internship Program offers diverse opportunities for students and recent graduates to gain professional experience across peace, health, and operational initiatives. Interns contribute directly to ongoing projects while developing skills relevant to careers in international affairs, nonprofit management, public health, and global policy.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must meet one of the following criteria:

  • Undergraduate students who have completed at least two years of study (minimum 60 credit hours in the U.S. system)

  • Graduate students currently enrolled or enrolled within the last 24 months

  • Recent graduates who completed their undergraduate degree within the past 24 months

Additional requirements include:

  • Authorization to work in the United States

  • No citizenship restrictions

  • International students enrolled at U.S.-based institutions may apply with approval from their host institution

All internship opportunities are based in Atlanta, Georgia.

Internship Compensation

Internships are paid through a stipend structure:

  • Spring Semester (15 weeks): $5,100

  • Summer Semester (10 weeks): $3,400

  • Fall Semester (15 weeks): $5,100

Internship Program Areas

Internship placements are available across three major divisions:

Peace Programs

  • China Focus(opens in new window)
  • Conflict Resolution Program(opens in new window)
  • Democracy(opens in new window)
  • Human Rights Program(opens in new window)
  • Inform Women, Transform Lives(opens in new window)
  • Latin America and Caribbean Focus(opens in new window)
  • Office of the Vice President of Peace(opens in new window)
  • Rule of Law(opens in new window)

Health Programs

  • International Public Health(opens in new window)
  • Mental Health Program(opens in new window)
  • Special Health Projects(opens in new window)

Operations Programs

  • Communications(opens in new window)
  • Development(opens in new window)
  • Educational Programs(opens in new window)
  • People Operations & Programs (Human Resources)(opens in new window)
  • Information Technology(opens in new window)
  • Strategy, Innovation and Learning(opens in new window)
  • Volunteer Services/Art Collection(opens in new window)
  • Overseas Operations(opens in new window)
  • DEI & Internal Communications

 

Intern Experience and Career Impact

Participants often describe the internship as a launching point for professional growth. Interns gain exposure to international development leadership, apply academic knowledge in real contexts, and build practical skills valued across the nonprofit and policy sectors.

Former interns have contributed to projects related to election observation, governance, and regional initiatives while developing expertise that supports future graduate study and professional advancement.

Apply Today

Start your journey at The Carter Center by applying for an internship or graduate assistantship today.

Internship Application

Carter Center Graduate Assistantship Program

Program Overview

The Graduate Assistantship Program offers a limited number of funded opportunities for master’s and doctoral students seeking deeper engagement in research and program implementation. Graduate assistants work closely with program teams and contribute to substantive projects while balancing academic commitments.

Program Structure

  • Duration: 9–12 months

  • Workload: 20 hours per week

  • Location: Atlanta, Georgia

  • Compensation: $18.50 per hour

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Be enrolled in a master’s or doctoral program throughout the assistantship

  • Have completed at least two semesters of graduate-level coursework at the start of the program

  • Remain enrolled until the end of the assistantship

Students who graduate before the assistantship concludes are not eligible.

Graduate Assistantship Program Areas

Peace

  • Conflict Resolution(opens in new window)
  • Democracy Program(opens in new window)
  • Inform Women, Transform Lives(opens in new window)
  • China Focus(opens in new window)

Health

  • Mental Health Program(opens in new window)
  • Trachoma Control Program(opens in new window)

Operations

  • Development: Donor Landscape Analysis(opens in new window)
  • People Operations & Programs

Apply Today

Start your journey at The Carter Center by applying for an internship or graduate assistantship today.

Graduate Assistantship Application

Application Dates and Deadlines

Internships

Summer 2026 Session

  • Application Deadline: March 2, 2026 (11:59 p.m. ET)

  • Start Date: May 19, 2026

Fall 2026 Session

  • Application Deadline: June 15, 2026 (11:59 p.m. ET)

  • Start Date: August 25, 2026

Graduate Assistantships

  • Application Opens: February 13, 2026

  • Application Deadline: April 23, 2026 (11:59 p.m. ET)

  • Recommendation Letters Deadline: April 30, 2026

Why Apply to the Carter Center Educational Programs

These programs are highly valuable for individuals seeking careers in international development and global impact. Key benefits include:

  • Paid professional experience

  • Exposure to peacebuilding and global health initiatives

  • Opportunities to apply academic training in real-world contexts

  • Access to experienced mentors and professionals

  • Strong preparation for graduate study or careers in NGOs, policy, and international organizations

How to Apply and Contact Information

Applications are submitted through the Carter Center Educational Programs portal. Candidates can apply for internships or graduate assistantships depending on eligibility.

Apply Today

Start your journey at The Carter Center by applying for an internship or graduate assistantship today.

Internship Application
Graduate Assistantship Application

For questions and eligibility clarification:

Email: EducationalPrograms@CarterCenter.org

Conclusion

The Carter Center Educational Programs offer a competitive and practical pathway for students and early-career professionals committed to advancing peace and health worldwide. With paid positions, structured mentorship, and meaningful project experience, the internships and graduate assistantships provide strong preparation for long-term careers in international development and global leadership.

Applicants are encouraged to prepare early and submit strong, well-tailored applications before the deadlines.

Source: https://www.cartercenter.org/educational-programs/#dates

The post Carter Center Educational Programs 2026: Paid Internships and Graduate Assistantships (Complete Guide) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: NYC Teaching Fellows 2026: Become a Fully Certified NYC Public School Teacher by Fall 2026 (Starting Salary $71,314+)
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
Are you ready to launch a meaningful career in education while making a direct impact in high-need communities? The NYC Teaching Fellows Program 2026 offers a highly selective, fully supported pathway to become a certified teacher in New York City Public Schools by Fall 2026. Designed for talented professionals and recent graduates from diverse backgrounds, […]

The post NYC Teaching Fellows 2026: Become a Fully Certified NYC Public School Teacher by Fall 2026 (Starting Salary $71,314+) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you ready to launch a meaningful career in education while making a direct impact in high-need communities? The NYC Teaching Fellows Program 2026 offers a highly selective, fully supported pathway to become a certified teacher in New York City Public Schools by Fall 2026.

Designed for talented professionals and recent graduates from diverse backgrounds, the program prepares participants to teach in critical shortage subject areas such as math, science, special education, and bilingual education—while earning a full-time salary and working toward a subsidized master’s degree.

If you are passionate about educational equity, student success, and leadership in urban education, this opportunity could redefine your career trajectory.

About NYC Teaching Fellows

NYC Teaching Fellows is a rigorous alternative certification program operated by NYC Public Schools—the largest public school system in the United States, serving approximately 1 million students across more than 1,600 schools.

The program recruits individuals committed to addressing educational inequities in underserved communities. Fellows receive intensive training, ongoing coaching, and professional development while working full-time as teachers.

By Fall 2026, selected candidates will be placed in NYC Public Schools classrooms and begin their careers as certified educators.

Program Structure and Timeline

Summer 2026 – Intensive Pre-Service Training

Participants begin with a 7-week intensive summer training program designed to build foundational teaching competencies, including:

  • Instructional planning

  • Classroom management

  • Curriculum development

  • Student assessment strategies

  • Equity-centered pedagogy

Fellows receive a stipend during this training period.

September 2026 – Full-Time Teaching Placement

In Fall 2026, Fellows:

  • Begin teaching full-time in NYC Public Schools

  • Earn a starting salary of $71,314+ per year

  • Receive full employee benefits

  • Continue working toward initial teaching certification

Graduate Education Component

Participants simultaneously pursue a subsidized master’s degree in education at a partner institution.

This dual-track model allows Fellows to:

  • Gain hands-on classroom experience

  • Complete graduate coursework

  • Earn certification

  • Develop advanced pedagogical skills

Compensation and Benefits

The NYC Teaching Fellows program offers a competitive financial and professional package:

Starting Salary

$71,314+ per year as a full-time NYC Public Schools teacher

Summer Training Stipend

Participants receive financial support during the 7-week summer training period.

Full Benefits Package

  • Medical insurance

  • Dental insurance

  • Vision insurance

  • Pension plan

  • 401(k)

  • Commuter benefits

  • Tuition assistance

  • Student loan assistance

Professional Development

  • Ongoing mentorship

  • Coaching support

  • Access to a network of dedicated educators

  • Structured professional growth pathway

High-Need Subject Areas

NYC Teaching Fellows recruits candidates for shortage subject areas, including:

  • Mathematics

  • Science

  • Special Education

  • Bilingual Education

Specific subject eligibility requirements may apply. Applicants are encouraged to review subject-specific criteria HERE.

Who Should Apply?

NYC Teaching Fellows seeks candidates who demonstrate:

  • Commitment to educational equity

  • Resilience and adaptability

  • Growth mindset

  • Passion for student success

  • Willingness to work in high-need communities

Importantly, no prior teaching experience is required.

Eligibility Requirements

To apply for the Fall 2026 cohort, candidates must:

  • Hold a bachelor’s degree by May 31, 2026

  • Have a minimum undergraduate GPA of 2.5

  • Be fluent in written and spoken English

  • Have permanent authorization to work in the United States

  • Never have participated in a program leading to U.S. or Puerto Rico teacher certification

  • Meet additional subject-specific requirements where applicable

Why Choose NYC Public Schools?

NYC Public Schools is the largest public school system in the U.S., employing:

  • 75,000+ teachers

  • Thousands of counselors, social workers, administrators, and staff

The system reflects New York City’s vibrant diversity and is deeply committed to equity, inclusion, and excellence.

By joining NYC Teaching Fellows, participants become part of a workforce dedicated to ensuring every student is seen, supported, and set up to thrive.

Equal Opportunity Employer

The NYC Department of Education is committed to nondiscrimination and equal opportunity in employment and education. Opportunities are provided without regard to race, color, religion, ethnicity, national origin, citizenship status, age, marital status, disability, sexual orientation, gender, military status, or other protected characteristics.

How to Apply

Find the application link HERE.

Click HERE to find the program information.

Interested candidates are encouraged to apply early due to the competitive and highly selective nature of the program.

Discover more job opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post NYC Teaching Fellows 2026: Become a Fully Certified NYC Public School Teacher by Fall 2026 (Starting Salary $71,314+) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
